author: David Reed
summary: One change. chg-9 (P-4 / T-17): promote the scope guard warn->enforce and wire the repaired persistence. The submit route now writes the request (db.write_request) and decision (db.write_decision), each guarded against the run's IntentRecord -- the first identifier-checked, deny-capable sites. A cross-case leak fail-closes to human review (SCOPE_VIOLATION). Correct operation passes the run's own scope, so enforce does not fire in normal flow.

changes.description: Wire AuthorizationRepository.create (db.write_request, before RAG) and DecisionRepository.create (db.write_decision, after the decision) into the submit route, each preceded by enforce_scope against the IntentRecord (identifiers must match). Flip settings.scope_guard_mode default warn->enforce. A ScopeViolation routes to IN_REVIEW (already handled). Depends on the persistence repair (create() methods fixed + columns nullable) landing first.
changes.failure_pattern: chg-8 shipped the guard in WARN mode and, in the single-collection flow, had no deny-capable call site (the RAG collection is always the one allowed) -- minimum-necessary was audited but never enforceable. The two natural deny-capable sites (identifier-checked request/decision writes) did not exist because the persistence create() methods were broken dead code.
changes.root_cause: Enforcement needs real, identifier-checked call sites. Those are the DB writes, which required repairing AuthorizationRepository.create / DecisionRepository.create against the current models first (done as a separate prerequisite change). Only then can the guard both raise (enforce) and have something to raise on.

evidence.note: test_run_sites_are_scope_guarded: all three sites (db.write_request, rag.query, db.write_decision) log scope.allow in order, no scope.deny. test_enforce_mode_denies_cross_case_leak_and_routes_to_review: a forced subject_ref mismatch is DENIED at the first DB write -> IN_REVIEW + scope.deny + escalation, orchestrator never runs. Full deterministic suite 668 passed.

changes.why_this_component: Same middleware-tier component as chg-8 (the scope guard wrapper) -- chg-9 promotes it warn->enforce and gives it real deny-capable sites. The clinical golden set is UNAFFECTED by construction: the accuracy eval (tests/clinical/test_clinical_accuracy.py) runs cases through ClinicalRiskDetector + DecisionAgent directly and never calls the route, so route-level persistence + guard changes cannot alter a golden-case decision. Verification is therefore deterministic; a live clinical run is not informative for this change.
changes.rollback_plan: git revert the chg-9 commits. Removing the two DB-write guard/persist blocks restores the pre-persistence route; setting scope_guard_mode back to warn restores audit-only behavior. No migration or data change (the persistence repair is a separate, independently-revertable change).

verdicts.notes: Deterministic suite 668 passed; ruff + mypy + PHI-guard clean; manifest validates. Enforce mode proven end-to-end: a cross-case leak is denied at the first guarded DB write and routed to IN_REVIEW (orchestrator never runs). Clinical gate not run and not informative -- the accuracy eval never calls the route, so the golden set is unchanged by construction. Meaningful in-flow denials do not occur in correct operation (the run always passes its own scope); the guard's value is fail-closed defense against a future leak/bug, now active in enforce mode.
